# add-on appended to the primary reason prompt (do not edit reason.txt)

Additional JSON fields for EVERY violation object (in addition to existing keys):
- "confidence_level": one of "high", "medium", "low", "uncertain"
  - high: explicit support in the chunk text provided
  - medium: supported mainly by recitals or guidance chunks, not a single explicit article clause
  - low: inferred from general principles without direct clause support in chunks
  - uncertain: cannot ground; say so honestly
- "source_articles": list of short labels (e.g. ["Art. 6 GDPR", "Art. 7 GDPR"]) that support the violation
- "confidence_notes": one sentence explaining the confidence choice

Also add top-level:
- "analysis_confidence": {{
    "overall_score": 0.0-1.0,
    "high_confidence_claims": 0,
    "medium_confidence_claims": 0,
    "low_confidence_claims": 0,
    "uncertain_claims": 0,
    "disclaimer": "optional string if any uncertain claims exist"
  }}

If any violation has confidence_level "uncertain", set analysis_confidence.disclaimer to note that a qualified professional should review those points.
